question_text: Two planets have the same gravitational field strength of 10 N/kg. Planet A has a mass of 6 × 10²⁴ kg and Planet B has a mass of 3 × 10²⁴ kg. A 50 kg object is placed on each planet. Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The object weighs more on Planet A because Planet A has greater mass.

options.label: B
options.text: The object weighs the same on both planets because gravitational field strength is the same on both.

options.label: C
options.text: The object weighs more on Planet B because the smaller planet exerts stronger gravity on nearby objects.

options.label: D
options.text: The object's mass is different on each planet, so its weight must be different.

explanation: Weight depends on both mass and gravitational field strength, calculated using W = mg. Since both planets have g = 10 N/kg and the object has the same mass (50 kg) on each planet, the weight is 50 × 10 = 500 N on both. The planet's total mass doesn't directly affect the gravitational field strength at its surface in this case—what matters is the field strength itself.
